executiveSummary.text: Risk tone shifted from momentum-rally to risk-off digestion: S&P 500 -1.0%, Nasdaq -1.4%, Dow -0.8%, VIX +12.2% to 18.77; semis were the pressure point rather than a broad credit/liquidity break.
Rates remain a valuation headwind: latest FRED 2Y 4.16%, 10Y 4.57%, 30Y 5.09%, 10Y real 2.35%; DXY was flat at 100.75 (+0.0%).

AI/semis saw a genuine narrative stress test: chip stocks sold off on AI capex/payback concerns and a fresh Chinese AI model headline; this is valuation/positioning risk more than evidence that long-run compute scarcity is broken.

Energy is the cleanest macro transmission channel from geopolitics: WTI +3.6% to 81.77, Brent +4.6% to 88.09 as Hormuz/Red Sea supply-route risk lifted the oil premium; 2nd order risk is inflation breakevens and long-end yields.
KL-specific abnormal moves: SNPS -7.9%, AMAT -5.6%, KLAC -3.0%, QQQ -1.5%, COPX -1.8%; classify as HOLD/WATCH, not thesis damage, unless hyperscaler capex guidance or semicap order books roll over.

regime.label: Risk-off digestion inside an AI-led bull trend; oil/geopolitics and real yields are the key macro constraints

usGlobal.what: US indices fell with tech/semis leading downside
usGlobal.why: Friday live-market reports and price data show S&P -1.0%, Nasdaq -1.4%, VIX +12%; selling concentrated in AI/semiconductor leaders and Netflix-related sentiment rather than systemic de-risking. Scenario weights: 55% healthy valuation reset, 30% broader rotation, 15% growth scare. Confidence: medium.
usGlobal.implication: Do not chase weakness indiscriminately; reserve adds for quality names where thesis is intact and valuation compression, not fundamentals, explains the move.

ratesMacro.what: Long rates and real yields remain high enough to cap duration equity multiples
ratesMacro.why: FRED latest shows 10Y 4.57%, 30Y 5.09%, 10Y real yield 2.35%; breakevens ticked to 2.24%. Oil shock raises the risk that nominal yields do not fall even if growth cools.
ratesMacro.implication: Quality cash flow and pricing power matter; REITs need clear evidence of yield relief before aggressive adds.

asiaSingapore.what: Singapore remains ballast but not immune to oil/rates pressure
asiaSingapore.why: Singapore banks slipped modestly (DBS -0.7%, OCBC -0.8%, UOB -2.4%) while REITs were mixed; fresh Singapore REIT commentary flagged oil/inflation/rates sensitivity and MLT China divestment friction remains a background concern.
asiaSingapore.implication: Banks still hedge higher-for-longer; REIT adds should be selective, balance-sheet led, and paced rather than broad.

commodities.what: Oil jumped; metals were more mixed
commodities.why: Brent +4.6%, WTI +3.6% on Persian Gulf/Hormuz and Red Sea route concerns. Gold rose +0.9% while copper fell -0.4%; this is not a simple 'geopolitical risk = all commodities up' tape—USD was flat and real yields remain elevated.
commodities.implication: Energy inflation is the macro risk; copper weakness suggests growth/China/positioning worries are offsetting scarcity narratives near term.

geopolitics.what: Middle East escalation has a plausible market transmission path through oil, shipping insurance and inflation expectations
geopolitics.why: CNBC/Reuters-linked reports said oil rose as US-Iran hostilities and attacks near Persian Gulf infrastructure increased concern around Hormuz/Red Sea supply routes; OilPrice reported geopolitical risk premium returning.
geopolitics.implication: Watch for sustained Brent >$90–95: 2nd order is inflation breakevens; 3rd order is Fed cuts delayed and long-duration equities/REITs derated.

moves.why: Benzinga/Investing.com attributed the drop to broad tech/semicap selloff plus concern from an AI-designed-chip demonstration that challenges parts of the EDA moat narrative; TradingKey also cited Ansys antitrust and cyclical end-market worries.
moves.implication: Thesis impact: WATCH, not broken. 2nd order: if AI can automate parts of design, EDA value may shift from seat-based tools to verified flows/IP/signoff; 3rd order: incumbents with trust, verification, IP and foundry integration may still capture value.
moves.action: WATCH — hold core; add only if management/reputable foundry customers confirm AI design augments rather than commoditizes SNPS workflows. Invalidate if renewal/pricing or Ansys deal economics deteriorate.

moves.what: Applied Materials fell -5.6% and KLA fell -3.0%
moves.why: Semiconductor equipment sold off with the broader chip rout as investors questioned AI capex durability and China/export-control exposure; KLAC crossed the abnormal-move threshold.
moves.implication: Mainly valuation/positioning noise unless foundry/memory capex orders weaken. 2nd order: equipment weakness pressures the AI scarcity sleeve; 3rd order: if fabs delay tools, HBM/advanced-node tightness may actually persist longer, supporting leaders later.
moves.action: HOLD / stagger ADD on deeper drawdowns; trigger: order backlog intact and -10–15% from recent highs. Invalidate: synchronized capex cuts from TSMC/Samsung/SK Hynix/Micron.

moves.what: QQQ fell -1.5%; TSM -2.8%, NVDA -2.2%, META -2.8%, GOOG -2.2%
moves.why: Large-cap AI complex repriced lower with VIX up and semis weak; no single holding-specific thesis break identified in the last-24h scan.
moves.implication: KL’s deliberate NVDA underweight helps relative regret control; hyperscalers remain judged by AI capex monetization in upcoming earnings.
moves.action: HOLD — no chase. Add only after earnings confirm cloud/AI demand and capex discipline.

moves.what: COPX fell -1.8% while copper futures fell -0.4%
moves.why: Growth/China and risk-off pressure outweighed the longer-term electrification/grid scarcity story.
moves.implication: Thesis intact but cyclical beta is high; copper miners can lag physical scarcity when risk appetite fades.
moves.action: HOLD / ADD only on copper demand confirmation or miner-specific free-cash-flow support; invalidate if China/property/industrial demand deteriorates further.

thesisImpact.why: AI inputs (compute, EDA, tools, power, copper, uranium) sold off unevenly while energy scarcity rose. This is a cross-current, not a single broken thesis.
thesisImpact.implication: Keep the barbell: cash dry powder + diversified AI supply chain beats forced concentration in a single AI winner.
thesisImpact.action: HOLD with selective adds after evidence, not headlines.

narratives.title: AI capex durability moves from unquestioned tailwind to earnings-season exam
narratives.description: Market is beginning to separate necessary AI infrastructure from overpaid AI exposure. Probability: 60% capex remains high but investors demand clearer ROI; 25% temporary positioning washout; 15% genuine AI capex slowdown.
narratives.priceImpact: multiple compression until hyperscaler guidance confirms ROI

narratives.title: Oil shock is the macro variable that can contaminate rates
narratives.description: If oil remains elevated, breakevens rise first; then nominal yields stay sticky; then duration assets and REITs face renewed pressure even without a growth boom.
narratives.source: CNBC/Reuters and OilPrice last-24h oil reports
narratives.priceImpact: negative for consumers/duration; positive for energy cash flows

narratives.title: Singapore defensive income is useful but not a free lunch
narratives.description: Banks benefit from higher-for-longer margins, while REITs face funding-cost and inflation pass-through tests. The right Singapore exposure is quality balance sheets, not blind yield chasing.
narratives.source: Yahoo Singapore / Business Times / market data last 24h where available

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil > inflation expectations > fewer Fed cuts / higher term premium > pressure on Nasdaq multiples and Singapore REIT discount rates.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Do not interpret oil risk as automatically bullish gold or bearish equities; the decisive path is through real yields, USD and liquidity.

secondOrderEffects.text: AI-chip selloff > semicap equipment derating > potential capex discipline narrative > if supply additions slow, HBM/advanced-node scarcity can persist, later supporting incumbents with pricing power.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Equipment drawdowns may become opportunities if order books remain intact.

secondOrderEffects.text: AI-designed-chip headlines > perceived EDA moat risk > incumbents may respond by embedding AI into signoff/verification/IP flows.
secondOrderEffects.implication: SNPS risk is business-model evolution, not immediate obsolescence; monitor pricing and customer stickiness.

investment.thesis: Keep accumulating diversified AI infrastructure scarcity, but demand evidence after the selloff
investment.trigger: Add in tranches if ASML/LRCX/AMAT/KLAC/SNPS fall another 5–10% while TSMC/hyperscaler capex guidance remains firm.
investment.whyNow: Valuation pressure is returning, but fundamentals have not yet clearly broken.
investment.invalidator: Broad capex cuts, order cancellations, or evidence EDA pricing power erodes.

investment.thesis: Maintain Singapore ballast via banks and only highest-quality REITs
investment.trigger: Add REITs only if 10Y yield moves decisively lower or spreads widen enough to compensate funding risk.
investment.whyNow: Rates and oil inflation risk argue for patience; banks still hedge the portfolio.
investment.invalidator: Credit stress, rising NPLs, or persistent high real yields.

trading.thesis: Do not buy the first red candle in semis blindly
trading.trigger: Wait for VIX stabilization below ~17 or a reversal in SOXX/QQQ breadth before tactical adds.
trading.whyNow: Friday selling looks positioning-heavy but can overshoot into earnings.
trading.invalidator: VIX continues above 20 with credit spreads widening.

trading.thesis: Oil shock hedge is useful only while route risk is real
trading.trigger: If Brent sustains above $90 with fresh shipping disruption, energy exposure/hedges outperform; if shipping normalizes, fade the premium.
trading.whyNow: The move has a clear geopolitical transmission path but may reverse sharply on de-escalation.
trading.invalidator: Confirmed reopening/normalization of Hormuz/Red Sea flows.

watchlist.thesis: Hyperscaler earnings are the next truth serum for AI capex
watchlist.trigger: MSFT/AMZN/GOOG/META capex + cloud revenue + AI monetization commentary.
watchlist.whyNow: Market is questioning ROI after a long AI run.
watchlist.invalidator: Capex stays high but cloud growth/AI revenue disappoints.

watchlist.thesis: Fed reaction function could shift if oil lifts inflation expectations
watchlist.whyNow: This is the path from geopolitics to equity multiples.

riskWatch.name: Oil/geopolitical inflation shock
riskWatch.description: Hormuz/Red Sea risk is now directly visible in Brent/WTI. Main danger is not just energy equities; it is inflation expectations and term premium.
riskWatch.mitigation: Keep cash ballast; avoid over-adding REITs/duration until yields confirm relief.

riskWatch.name: AI valuation air pocket
riskWatch.description: Semis and EDA can fall further if earnings guidance does not validate capex expectations.
riskWatch.mitigation: Stagger buys; favor monopolistic toolchains and profitable hyperscalers over speculative AI beta.

riskWatch.name: Rates/real-yield persistence
riskWatch.description: 10Y real yield around 2.35% keeps pressure on long-duration assets.
riskWatch.mitigation: Balance growth sleeves with cash, Singapore banks, and quality dividend exposure.

keyEvents.text: US stocks posted weekly losses; Nasdaq fell more than 1% Friday as chip stocks dragged the market lower.
Oil rose Friday as US-Iran hostilities and Persian Gulf infrastructure attacks raised Hormuz/Red Sea supply-route concerns.
Synopsys sold off sharply; reports cited broader tech weakness plus concerns around AI-designed-chip demonstrations and Ansys/cyclical worries.
Singapore REIT commentary highlighted oil/inflation sensitivity; MLT China divestment concerns remain a background issue but not a fresh 24h headline.

bottomLine.whatMatters: Whether AI weakness stays a valuation reset or becomes an earnings/capex downgrade; whether oil risk feeds breakevens and real/nominal yields.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Keep the barbell. Hold core AI scarcity assets, use cash only in tranches after confirmation, and be patient with REITs until rate relief is clearer.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: One-day panic narratives that confuse positioning drawdowns with thesis failure; simplistic “geopolitics means gold up” claims without checking USD, real yields and liquidity.
